title: Bharat Aluminium Co vs Kaiser Aluminium Technical Services
summary: A landmark judgment where the Supreme Court held that Part I of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996 does not apply to international commercial arbitrations held outside India. The court overruled its earlier decision in Bhatia International and held that Indian courts cannot intervene in foreign-seated arbitrations unless parties expressly agree otherwise.
relevantSections: Section 2 of Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996
Section 45 of Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996

title: Fuerst Day Lawson Ltd vs Jindal Exports Ltd
summary: The Supreme Court held that a foreign arbitral award can be enforced directly under Part II of the Arbitration Act without a separate suit. The court clarified the procedure for enforcement of New York Convention awards in India and held that the executing court can directly enforce the award.
relevantSections: Section 48 of Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996
Section 49 of Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996

title: Gemini Bay Transcription Pvt Ltd vs Integrated Sales Service Limited
summary: The Supreme Court held that a foreign arbitral award can be enforced against non-signatories to the arbitration agreement if they are bound by the award under the law of the seat of arbitration. The court clarified that the enforcement court need not re-examine the merits of the award.
relevantSections: Section 44 of Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996
Section 46 of Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996
Section 48 of Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996

title: GE Power Conversion India Pvt Ltd vs PASL Wind Solutions Pvt Ltd
summary: The Supreme Court held that two Indian parties can choose a foreign seat of arbitration and their agreement is valid under Indian law. The court upheld party autonomy and ruled that such awards can be enforced in India as foreign awards under Part II of the Arbitration Act.
relevantSections: Section 44 of Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996

title: Fortune Infrastructure vs Trevor D Lima
summary: The Supreme Court held that a buyer cannot be made to wait indefinitely for possession of property by a builder. In cases of unreasonable delay in offering possession, the buyer is entitled to seek full refund with compensation and cannot be compelled to accept possession at a belated stage.

title: Kailash Nath Associates vs Delhi Development Authority
summary: The Supreme Court held that forfeiture of earnest money must be a genuine pre-estimate of damages and cannot be a penalty. The court ruled that if actual loss is not proved, the party cannot forfeit the entire earnest money and must refund the excess amount beyond actual damages suffered.

title: Innoventive Industries Ltd vs ICICI Bank
summary: The Supreme Court in this landmark judgment interpreted the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code 2016 and held that once a moratorium is declared under IBC, all pending suits and proceedings against the corporate debtor must be stayed. The court clarified the overriding effect of IBC over other laws.
relevantSections: Section 13 of Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code 2016
Section 14 of Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code 2016
Section 238 of Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code 2016

title: Mobilox Innovations Pvt Ltd vs Kirusa Software Pvt Ltd
summary: The Supreme Court laid down the law for operational creditors under IBC and held that if there is a pre-existing dispute about the debt, the insolvency application must be rejected. The court clarified the meaning of dispute and the threshold for admitting insolvency petitions by operational creditors.
relevantSections: Section 8 of Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code 2016
Section 9 of Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code 2016

title: Larsen and Toubro Limited vs Experion Developers Pvt Ltd
summary: The Supreme Court upheld the arbitration clause in a construction contract between L&T and Experion Developers. The court held that disputes arising out of EPC contracts are arbitrable and directed the parties to resolve their disputes through arbitration as per the contract.
relevantSections: Section 11 of Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996
Section 8 of Arbitration and Conciliation Act 1996

title: L&T Hydrocarbon Engineering Ltd vs Indian Oil Corporation Limited
summary: The Supreme Court examined the termination of an engineering and construction contract between L&T Hydrocarbon and Indian Oil Corporation. The court held that wrongful termination of contract entitles the contractor to damages including loss of profit and costs incurred before termination.

title: Godrej Properties and Investments Ltd vs Tripura Construction
summary: The Bombay High Court granted specific performance of a development agreement between Godrej Properties and a construction company. The court held that where monetary compensation is not adequate remedy for breach of a property development contract, specific performance must be granted.

title: Jute Corporation of India Ltd vs ABL International Ltd
summary: The Calcutta High Court directed recovery of money in a commercial trade dispute between Jute Corporation and an international trading company. The court held that where goods are supplied and payment is not made, the supplier is entitled to recover the amount with interest at commercial rates.

title: Maxlinear Technologies Pvt Ltd vs Aptamitra Consulting Pvt Ltd
summary: The Delhi High Court ruled in favor of the plaintiff in a consulting services payment dispute. The court held that where services are rendered as per contract and payment is withheld without valid reason, the service provider is entitled to full payment along with interest and costs.

title: Union of India vs Indian Agro Marketing Cooperative
summary: The Supreme Court examined a contract dispute between the Union of India and an agricultural marketing cooperative. The court held that government contracts must be performed in good faith and that wrongful breach entitles the affected party to damages even when the other party is the government.
